codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, completed-worker handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and current `src/model/calibration.py` / `tests/test_calibration.py` implementation. The fix now supports configurable probability calibrators, keeps the dedicated chronological calibration split, and the handoff shows the real 2025 holdout run meeting the requested Brier/ECE/reliability targets with Platt scaling. I did not find a remaining blocking defect in the touched code, but there is one non-blocking metadata edge case.

issues.description: If `_fit_probability_calibrator()` falls back to `IdentityProbabilityCalibrator` for a single-class calibration slice (`src/model/calibration.py:691-692`), `train_calibrated_models()` still writes the requested `calibration_method` into the run summary/result (`src/model/calibration.py:289` and `:308`). That makes `calibration_run_*.json` and the CLI JSON misreport which calibrator actually shipped, which could hide degenerate calibration slices from downstream tooling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared startup commands are still wrong for this Windows repo workflow: `init_windows` contains the broken `init_db(''data/mlb.db'')` quoting, and `.factory/init.sh` still relies on bare `pip`, so workers continue to bypass the documented init helpers manually.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/services.yaml:7` contains `init_db(''data/mlb.db'')`; `.factory/init.sh:24` runs bare `pip install -e ".[dev]"`; the handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-20T00-12-56-791Z__t19-isotonic-calibration__86929812-1fe2-47b3-a5c7-2540b60cc879.json:49-67` records the resulting deviation and workaround.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `ml-worker` skill manual-verification step is too generic for calibration-gate fixes: it still prescribes a subset train plus short backtest, while this feature needed the real `src.model.calibration` 2025 holdout evaluation to prove the acceptance criteria.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/ml-worker/SKILL.md:41-44` says to train on a subset and run a short backtest; the handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-20T00-12-56-791Z__t19-isotonic-calibration__86929812-1fe2-47b3-a5c7-2540b60cc879.json:64-67` explains why the worker had to deviate and run the full holdout calibration check instead.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared calibration library note is stale after this fix: it still says isotonic calibration is blocked by 0.15-0.17 reliability gaps, even though the completed fix switched the default path to Platt scaling and the verified 2025 holdout run passed all three gates.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/ml-calibration.md:1-10` still describes the unresolved isotonic blocker; the handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-20T00-12-56-791Z__t19-isotonic-calibration__86929812-1fe2-47b3-a5c7-2540b60cc879.json:10-11` and `:36-38` record the new Platt-based implementation and passing holdout metrics.

summary: Pass. Commit `4e163f9` resolves the feature goal by moving calibration to a configurable method set and using Platt scaling to meet the 2025 holdout Brier, ECE, and reliability-gap gates on both F5 models. I found one non-blocking edge-case issue in run-level `calibration_method` reporting, and I noted shared-state gaps in the services manifest, the `ml-worker` skill, and the stale calibration library note.
